mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is suitable for applications requiring semantic search and matching, offering extensive filtering capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for careful resource management and potential complexity in configuration for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Qdrant CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Qdrant instances.

```bash

curl -sSfL https://get.qdrant.tech/cli.sh | sh

```

> This command downloads and installs the Qdrant CLI.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Qdrant cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

qdrant cluster create --version 1.13 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Qdrant cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kubeconfig.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct path to your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Qdrant cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Qdrant deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This configures autoscaling based on resource usage.
> This sets up monitoring for your Qdrant instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

qdrant cluster delete --force

```

> This command will delete the cluster and all its resources.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

```

> This will delete all persistent volume claims associated with the Qdrant cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and check the compatibility of your configurations.

```bash

qdrant backup create --all

qdrant cluster upgrade --version 1.13

kubectl get pods

```

> Create a backup of all data before proceeding.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Qdrant service is not running or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods with 'kubectl get pods' and ensure your kubeconfig is set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources available for the new pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce the resource requests for your pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database connection timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Qdrant service is overloaded or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the resource usage and logs of the Qdrant pods for any signs of overload.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration file'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the configuration file has syntax errors or unsupported settings.

**Fix:**
Validate your configuration file against the Qdrant documentation and fix any errors.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for advanced filtering options and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old snapshot storage method in favor of the new S3-compatible storage.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12, ensure to migrate your snapshot storage configuration.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
